text:
Once upon a time in a whimsical village nestled between mist-covered mountains, lived a girl named Clara. Clara had a magical mop that could clean any surface just by being near it; she didn't need to touch it. Everyone in the village loved Clara because not only did she have an incredible mop, but she also knew just how to make everything better.

One sunny morning, as Clara stood on a hill watching the clouds scroll by, she noticed something peculiar—a small bird stuck in a spider web. Unable to resist her nature-saving instincts, she went down to help.

After freeing the bird, she decided to make a small bouquet for its parent. She knew she couldn't make them feathers because the web was silk. Gathering pieces of cloth she found hanging around, she fashioned something delicate and colorful.

The villagers noticed the bouquet and gathered around Clara. She gifted it to the mother bird, whom they had nicknamed Webby. Webby perched on a branch, accepting the bouquet with fluttered
